devSite.heroName: Rules in your language. Decisions you can trust.
devSite.heroText: Write policies, workflows and decisions in plain {languages} — run them on a deterministic, auditable engine where every decision is replayable.
devSite.heroCodeHint: Real Aster source — run it in the Playground.
devSite.valueLanguageBody: Write rules in the natural language your team already speaks. No DSL ceremony, no operator soup — the source reads like the policy it encodes.
devSite.valueDeterministicBody: Two independent runtimes, one intermediate representation, byte-identical results. The same rule never disagrees with itself.
devSite.valueAuditableBody: Every decision carries a trace you can replay — which rule fired, on which inputs, to which result. Nothing hidden, nothing inferred.
devSite.businessExpertBody: Compliance officer, risk analyst, or policy author. You want rules in your language, with AI helping you write the first draft.
devSite.developerBody: You want a deterministic, auditable rules engine you can embed — two runtimes, one IR, every decision replayable.

playground.title: Playground
playground.subtitle: Write a rule, run it, replay the decision.
playground.traceHint: Which rule fired, on which inputs, to which result.

seo.title: Demos — Aster Lang
seo.description: Interactive demos: a playful cat-mood engine, a Texas hold’em showdown judge, and source-as-poem/deduction — all run the real engine in your browser.

demosIndex.title: See Aster in action
demosIndex.subtitle: Four live demos — each runs the real engine in your browser, no signup.

kitten.title: Cat mood engine 🐱
kitten.description: A playful one: a poetic cat-vocabulary rule drives a little hand-drawn cat animation. Proof that Aster rules read like plain language.

poker.title: Poker showdown 🃏
poker.description: A poker-vocabulary rule decides the winning hand at a self-dealing table — and awards the trophy. Same provable engine, just dealt in cards.

jingyesi.title: Quiet Night Thoughts
jingyesi.description: A Li Bai poem that is runnable Aster — outputs the poem’s title.

sherlock.title: The Speckled Band
sherlock.description: Sherlock’s deduction as a decision rule — feed clues, get the culprit.

seo.title: Poker showdown — Aster Lang
seo.description: A poker-vocabulary rule decides the winning hand at a self-dealing table. The real engine runs in your browser; the winner gets the trophy.

pokerPage.title: Poker showdown engine 🃏
pokerPage.subtitle: A rule written in poker words decides who wins — the same provable engine as the credit demo, just dealt in cards. The table deals itself, on a loop.
pokerPage.ruleTitle: The rule that decides the winner
pokerPage.legend: words are poker domain vocabulary, injected into the engine and compiled like any other rule.
pokerPage.handsHint: Each player makes their best five-card hand from their two cards plus the five on the table. The stronger hand wins.
pokerPage.footer: Domain vocabulary can be anything — credit, claims, or a hand of poker. The engine that deals this table is the same one that decides a loan.

seo.title: The Cat Mood Engine — a poetic Aster Lang demo
seo.description: Write a rule in cat-whisperer vocabulary, run it through the real engine, and watch a 5-second sketch cat react. Domain vocabulary, but make it whiskers.

catMoodPage.title: The Cat Mood Engine
catMoodPage.subtitle: A rule written in the sacred vocabulary of cat-whisperers — compiled and run by the very same engine that decides loans. The cat does not care, but it will react.
catMoodPage.poem:
Where the sunbeam pools and the bowl is full,
the small god decides what mood to rule.
No intruder? Then warmth, or a purr, or — pfft —
the slow blink of judgment, exquisitely cool.
catMoodPage.legend: = cat vocabulary the engine learned for this demo.

perch.name: 🐈 Perch
perch.quip: High ground secured. From this throne the whole domain is under surveillance. As it should be.

purr.name: 😌 Purr
purr.quip: Belly full, world forgiven. The motor is running. You may continue to exist.

loaf.name: 🍞 Loaf
loaf.quip: Warmth detected. Bones now optional. The cat has become bread.

floof.name: 🙀 Floof
floof.quip: A STRANGER. Double in size, triple in suspicion. This is fine. (It is not fine.)

judge.name: 😼 Judgment
judge.quip: No sun, no snacks, no patience. You are being evaluated. You did not pass.

catMoodPage.footer: Silly vocabulary, serious engine: this cat rule compiled and ran on the same Aster Lang runtime as a credit policy. Your words — even these — become an auditable decision.
catMoodPage.idleHint: The cat is doing its own thing. Pick a scene below to see how it reacts.

seo.title: Quiet Night Thoughts — Aster demo
seo.description: Li Bai’s poem is Aster source: run it and it outputs the poem’s title. Display layout decoupled from compile source via LayoutMap.

jingyesiPage.title: Quiet Night Thoughts — Li Bai
jingyesiPage.subtitle: The whole poem, in original word order, is runnable Aster. Two layouts of the same program — display reads as the poem, canonical is what compiles.
jingyesiPage.displayHint: Li Bai’s poem, its natural four lines.
jingyesiPage.canonicalHint: The canonical source (what actually compiles) — layout bent to Aster’s grammar.
jingyesiPage.decoupleNote: Same program, two layouts — display frees the poem, compile runs the canonical source (LayoutMap).
jingyesiPage.runLabel: Run the poem-as-program, it outputs the poem’s name:

seo.title: The Speckled Band — Aster demo
seo.description: Sherlock’s deduction as an Aster decision rule: feed clues, it outputs the culprit. Display reasoning decoupled from compile source via LayoutMap.

sherlockPage.title: The Speckled Band — Sherlock’s deduction
sherlockPage.subtitle: Sherlock’s reasoning is a runnable Aster decision rule. Two layouts — display reads as his monologue, canonical is the indented decision chain that compiles.
sherlockPage.displayHint: Sherlock’s reasoning, as a flowing monologue.
sherlockPage.canonicalHint: The canonical source (what compiles) — inline-if decision chain with then/else.
sherlockPage.decoupleNote: Same rule, two layouts — display reads as reasoning, compile runs the canonical chain (LayoutMap).
